title: Ability to remove config values
body:
**Description**

```buildah config``` allows to add various config options, but it seems like it is not possible to unset most of them with the command.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `buildah config --volume /test123 $containerid`
2. `buildah inspect $containerid`
3. `buildah config --volume [] $containerid`


**Describe the results you received:**
Help text indicates "[]" is the default for volume, but I'm not able to set it back to that nor am I able to remove certain volumes with ```buildah config --volume```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Command line option to remove a certain setting

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.5-2.gite94b4f9.el7.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 1.5 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server release 7.6 (Maipo)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux dc00-sans001t.helvetia.ch 3.10.0-957.5.1.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Dec 19 10:46:58 EST 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
I would be fine with this, but we need to think what the syntax would be.

We could do something like:
```
--volume='[]' --label='[]' --env='[]' --anotations='[]' --onbuild=[] --port='[]'
Or just add the keywork none to all flags to remove any settings?
```
Which would either remove the container config settings or all settings including those in the `from` image?

It's not the easiest thing to spot, but Kubernetes seems to have a convention that "setting" a value that ends with "-" actually unsets it.  A couple of the examples for [kubectl set env](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#-em-env-em-) and the last example in [kubectl label](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands#label) might be relevant here.

`kubernetes label` also seems to have an --overwrite option which clears the field and puts in the new value passed in.   I'm a little leery about the parsing work of having to check a number of options for a trailing '-'.

So then 
--volume=/source-
Would remove all mount points /source?
That's my read if you didn't use the `--overwrite` option.
@ashley-cui Could you see about implementing the `-` syntax.
